coverage_summary: The improved test cases show significant enhancement over the previous version. All core functional requirements (FR001), business rules (BR001, BR002), and validations (VAL001, VAL002) are now covered. Key gaps from the previous review have been addressed, including password confirmation mismatch, empty fields, SQL injection, and case-sensitive email uniqueness. However, coverage gaps remain for email format validation, password complexity, email verification, and maximum password length boundaries.
remaining_gaps: No test case for email format validation (e.g., missing '@', invalid domain, special characters in local part)
No test case for password complexity requirements (uppercase, lowercase, digit, special character) - not specified but identified as risk
No test case for email verification/confirmation flow (if required)
No test case for maximum password length boundary (if defined)
No test case for XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) in email or password fields
No test case for very long email or password inputs (buffer overflow / input length limits)
No test case for leading/trailing whitespace handling in email or password fields
recommendations: Add test cases for email format validation: missing '@', invalid domain (e.g., 'user@'), multiple '@' symbols, special characters in local part, and empty domain
Add test cases for password complexity: require at least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit, and one special character if implemented
Add test case for email verification flow: verify that account is not active until email is confirmed, and test resend verification link
Add boundary test for maximum password length if defined (e.g., 64 or 128 characters)
Add security test for XSS in email and password fields (e.g., '<script>alert(1)</script>')
Add test case for very long input strings (e.g., 1000+ characters) to check for buffer overflow or truncation behavior
Add test case for leading/trailing whitespace: verify that ' user@example.com ' is either trimmed or rejected consistently
Clarify expected error messages in test cases (e.g., exact wording for 'Email already registered' and 'Password must be at least 8 characters')
